name: Donald Harrison
abstract: Donald Harrison Jr. (born June 23, 1960) is an American jazz saxophonist and the Big Chief of The Congo Square Nation Afro-New Orleans Cultural Group from New Orleans, Louisiana.

concerts.label: Kool Jazz Festival: Young New Orleans
JVC Jazz Festival: Tito Puente's Golden Latin Jazz All-Stars/ Eddie Palmieri and His Afro-Cuban Jazz Octet
Lena Horne with the Count Basie Orchestra
Lena Horne with the Count Basie Orchestra
Carnegie Hall Jazz Band: An Evening of Irving Berlin and Cole Porter

works.t: Kiss Me, Kate: So In Love

about_quotes.speaker: Branford Marsalis
about_quotes.evidence: Me, Donald and Terrence were roommates. And then we went to another room and it was me, Donald, and Smitty were roommates... Me, Victor Bailey, Donald Harrison, Smitty Smith, the principle guys who went... We hopped a train and we went to New York... We played a bunch of tunes.
about_quotes.doc_title: Branford Marsalis — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Arturo O'Farrill
about_quotes.evidence: that’s actually a piece that we recorded on a record that I love... the last cut on “The Offense of the Drum” is that arrangement of “Iko Iko” with Donald Harrison.
about_quotes.doc_title: Arturo O'Farrill interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, May 6, 2024

about_quotes.speaker: Benny Golson
about_quotes.evidence: The last time we did it, I think it was Terence Blanchard and Donald Harrison. They were in the group then.
about_quotes.doc_title: Benny Golson — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Marian McPartland
about_quotes.evidence: First, I met Wynton and Branford [Marsalis] and Donald Harrison when I was in New Orleans.
about_quotes.doc_title: Marian McPartland — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Carl Allen
about_quotes.evidence: he and I played together with Terence Blanchard and Donald Harrison.
about_quotes.doc_title: Carl Allen interviewed by Monk Rowe, Atlanta, Georgia, January 4, 2013

about_quotes.speaker: Kent Jordan
about_quotes.evidence: Donald Harrison, try it yourself. Put on, I don’t care, you can put on a thousand CDs for some of your, you know like people who really don’t follow the music, or people who follow the music, and I’ll guarantee you they could not tell you, I mean like the newer stuff, who it really is.
about_quotes.doc_title: Kent Jordan interviewed by Monk Rowe, Rome, New York, August 22, 2006
